sources.title: Nevada Board of Wildlife Commissioners, CR 25-16, Fishing Seasons and Regulations (Jan. 1, 2026 – Dec. 31, 2027) — Washoe County, general "All waters" listing
sources.quotedText: All waters (Except as noted below). Open year around. Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye. Any hour of the day or night

sources.title: CR 25-16 — Washoe County CLOSED WATERS and special-water listings (Lewers Creek is not named among the closed or specially-regulated waters — Lake Tahoe tributaries, Marlette Lake, or Hobart Reservoir/Franktown Creek)
sources.quotedText: CLOSED WATERS: Third Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Incline Creek and its tributaries from Lake Tahoe to Ski Way and the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. Wood Creek from Lake Tahoe to the Mt. Rose Highway No. 431. ... Hobart Reservoir and tributaries and Franktown Creek downstream from Hobart Reservoir to Red House. May 1 through October 31. ... Marlette Lake, tributaries and outlet stream. July 15 through September 30. Daily: 0 trout, catch and release only.

sources.title: Lewers Creek, Nevada — TopoQuest (USGS Geographic Names Information System / USGS 1:24K topo, "Carson City" quad)
sources.quotedText: Location: N39.236298° W119.842963° ... Elevation: 5200 ft MSL ... County: Washoe County, Nevada ... USGS Topographic Map: Carson City, NV (1:24K scale) ... cataloged in the USGS Geographic Names Information System with ID 860330

sources.title: Apply & Buy — Nevada Fishing Licenses — NDOW
sources.quotedText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.

parameters.note: Lewers Creek is a small east-slope Carson Range stream in Washoe County, Nevada. USGS/GNIS places its mouth at N39.236298 W119.842963, elevation ~5,200 ft, on the west edge of Washoe Valley near Franktown and Lakeview — it drains EAST toward Washoe Valley (the Washoe Lake / Franktown Creek side of the range), NOT west toward Lake Tahoe, and it lies well north of, and in a separate drainage from, the Marlette Lake and Hobart Reservoir basins to the south (Marlette Lake ≈ 39.174, -119.902; Hobart Reservoir ≈ 39.194, -119.866). It is therefore not a Lake Tahoe tributary, not a Marlette Lake tributary/outlet, and not a Hobart Reservoir tributary or Franktown Creek itself (Hobart-to-Red House). It is not named individually anywhere in CR 25-16 and is not in Washoe County's closed-waters list, so the applicable rule is the county's general 'All waters (Except as noted below)' listing — open year around. This is an interpretive determination from the creek's course/position; verify against current NDOW guidance.

regulations.humanSummary: Open year-round (Nevada / Washoe County general 'All waters' listing; Lewers Creek is a Washoe Valley east-slope stream that is not individually listed and is not in the county closed-waters list).
regulations.verbatimText: All waters (Except as noted below). Open year around.

parameters.counting_rule: Washoe County general limit: Daily 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish, and 15 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye); Possession 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish, and 30 warmwater game fish (of which not more than 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye). On a small trout stream the practical limit is 5 trout per day / 10 in possession.

regulations.humanSummary: 5 trout daily / 10 in possession (plus the county's separate mountain-whitefish and warmwater allowances) under the Washoe County general 'All waters' limit.
regulations.verbatimText: Daily: 5 trout, 5 mountain whitefish and 15 warmwater game fish of which not more than 5 may be black bass and 5 may be walleye. Possession: 10 trout, 10 mountain whitefish and 30 warmwater gamefish of which 10 may be black bass and 10 may be walleye.

parameters.note: Under the Washoe County general 'All waters' listing, fishing is permitted any hour of the day or night (the restricted 1-hour-before-sunrise-to-2-hours-after-sunset window applies only to the county's Lake Tahoe, Marlette, and Hobart/Franktown special rows, none of which cover Lewers Creek).

regulations.humanSummary: Fishing is allowed any hour of the day or night.
regulations.verbatimText: Any hour of the day or night

parameters.note: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers 12 and older. Lewers Creek is a Nevada (Washoe Co.) inland stream, so a Nevada license — not the Lake Tahoe CA/NV reciprocal arrangement, which is written for fishing on the lake itself — applies.

regulations.humanSummary: A Nevada fishing license is required for anglers age 12 and older; none required under 12.
regulations.verbatimText: Anyone 12 years of age or older is required to have a fishing license in their possession while in the field.
